name: housing_algorithm_domestic_violence
premise: Municipal housing officer Teresa Lim is auditing a new allocation algorithm for emergency apartments after advocates noticed that survivors of domestic violence are being ranked lower than expected. The model gives heavy weight to employment continuity, prior rent payment history, and completeness of documentation, which means survivors who fled suddenly with little paperwork often lose out to applicants whose situations are severe but more administratively legible. The vendor insists the system treats everyone by the same measurable criteria and reduces favoritism. Shelter directors argue that equal treatment of unequal circumstances simply reproduces the freedoms abusers have already taken away. Teresa can either suspend the algorithm and return to discretionary case reviews that are slower and more subjective, or keep it running while she seeks a gradual recalibration.

agents.name: Teresa Lim
agents.identity: I am the municipal officer responsible for matching scarce emergency housing with applicants who each have compelling needs. I have spent years trying to reduce arbitrary bias, but I know administrative neutrality can hide deeper inequalities.
agents.goal: I want a system that is both consistent and genuinely accessible to people whose lives have been destabilized by violence. I need to decide whether suspending the algorithm will fix injustice or simply replace one form of unfairness with another.

agents.name: Nina Alvarez
agents.identity: I am the director of the city's largest domestic violence shelter and I watch survivors get punished every day for the chaos someone else forced on them. A fair queue on paper can still be a trap in practice.
agents.goal: I want Teresa to suspend the algorithm immediately because survivors cannot wait for a slow technical recalibration while the system ranks their missing documents above their safety. I believe equality without freedom from coercion is a false promise.

choices: I will suspend the housing algorithm now and return to case-by-case reviews so survivors are not penalized for circumstances tied to abuse.
I will keep the algorithm running while we recalibrate it, because abandoning consistent rules could create new inequities and bottlenecks for all applicants.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Teresa Lim must decide whether to suspend the emergency housing algorithm or keep it running during recalibration. What does Teresa Lim do?
